text: Screening recommendation for adults 18 and older: The USPSTF recommends screening for hypertension in adults 18 years or older with office blood pressure measurement (OBPM).
anchor_quote: The USPSTF recommends screening for hypertension in adults 18 years or older with office blood pressure measurement (OBPM)

text: Out-of-office confirmation before diagnosis/treatment: The USPSTF recommends obtaining blood pressure measurements outside of the clinical setting for diagnostic confirmation before starting treatment.
anchor_quote: The USPSTF recommends obtaining blood pressure measurements outside of the clinical setting for diagnostic confirmation before starting treatment

text: Annual screening interval for higher-risk adults: Screening for hypertension every year in adults 40 years or older and in adults at increased risk for hypertension (such as Black persons, persons with high-normal blood pressure, or persons who are overweight or obese).

text: Lower-frequency interval for younger low-risk adults: Screening less frequently (ie, every 3 to 5 years) as appropriate for adults aged 18 to 39 years not at increased risk for hypertension and with a prior normal blood pressure reading.
anchor_quote: Screening less frequently (ie, every 3 to 5 years) as appropriate for adults aged 18 to 39 years not at increased risk for hypertension and with a prior normal

text: Risk factors identifying increased-risk patients: Risk factors that increase a person's risk for the condition include older age, Black race, family history, excess weight and obesity, lifestyle habits (lack of physical activity, stress, and tobacco use), and dietary factors (diet high in fat or sodium, diet low in potassium, or excessive alcohol intake).
